channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Inflation Risks and Strategies to Protect Retirement Savings
item.description: A potential rise in inflation, driven by geopolitical tensions such as the Iran conflict, could have a significant impact on retirees’ financial security. Even a moderate increase of around 1.5 per ce...

item.title: FIFA Requires Female Coaches in Nigerian Women’s Football Teams
item.description: FIFA don set new rule wey go affect all women football teams for their competitions. As per the new directive, every women team must get female representation for key coaching and technical roles. Thi...

item.title: Rising global oil prices strain African economies despite limited gains for producers
item.description: A sharp increase in global oil prices, triggered by conflict in the Middle East, is placing significant pressure on African economies, according to a group of scholars from across the continent. The s...

item.title: Lidl ordered to pay £45,000 to former deputy store manager with ADHD after unfair dismissal ruling
item.description: A former Lidl employee, Ryan Toghill, has been awarded £45,147 in compensation after an employment tribunal ruled that his dismissal for gross misconduct was unfair. Mr Toghill, who worked as a shift ...

item.title: Separating Politics from Everyday Kenyan Life
item.description: The opinion piece argues that politics has infiltrated every aspect of Kenyan life, much like smoke filling the lungs, making it hard to breathe freely. The author likens excessive political discourse...

item.title: Fact Check: No truth to claim that Batangas Rep. Gerville Luistro was removed from office
item.description: A viral social media post from March 10, 2026 claimed that Batangas 2nd District Representative Gerville Luistro was removed from her position in the House of Representatives. The post gained signific...

item.title: Stellantis postpones Coega assembly plant opening to 2028 and plans to produce three models including a hybrid
item.description: Stellantis South Africa has revised its plans for a new vehicle assembly plant in the Coega Special Economic Zone in the Eastern Cape, delaying the start of production by two years to mid-2028. Origin...

item.title: Analysis of Canadian Government Communication Failures Regarding Kuwaiti Base Attack
item.description: Recent reports have revealed that an Iranian missile struck a Kuwaiti military facility, placing personnel from multiple nations, including members of the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF), at risk. The Dep...

item.title: Israel Commits to Halting Further Strikes on Iranian Energy Sites After Netanyahu-Trump Coordination
item.description: The US-Israel war against Iran has entered its third week, with significant developments in energy infrastructure attacks and global economic repercussions. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu a...

item.title: Parliament Pushes for Quick ICT Backbone Project Between Tanzania and DR Congo
item.description: The Parliamentary Committee on Infrastructure in Tanzania has urged the government to fast-track the construction of a national ICT backbone connecting Tanzania and the Democratic Republic of the Cong...

item.title: Hontiveros asks DBM to expand cash assistance for PUV drivers impacted by fuel price surge
item.description: Senate Deputy Majority Leader Risa Hontiveros has appealed to Budget Secretary Rolando Toledo to broaden the government’s financial assistance to include more public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers, ope...

item.title: West Bengal government challenges Election Commission’s transfer of senior officers in High Court ahead of assembly polls
item.description: The Trinamool Congress (TMC)-led West Bengal government has approached the Calcutta High Court challenging the Election Commission of India’s decision to transfer several senior IAS and IPS officers f...

item.title: Two-pot retirement system highlights inequality between middle class and low-income South Africans
item.description: This reader’s letter reflects on the recently released data around South Africa’s two-pot retirement savings system, arguing that public interpretation of the figures misses a deeper socio-economic re...

item.title: Police deny hijacking claims following high-speed chase and gunfire in KuGompo City
item.description: Police in KuGompo City, formerly known as East London, have dismissed widespread social media rumours and eyewitness accounts claiming a hijacking took place during a dramatic incident on Thursday aft...

item.title: Meningitis Outbreak Claims Teen's Life, Sparks Vaccine Concerns in Kent
item.description: A tragic meningitis outbreak in Kent has claimed the life of an 18-year-old girl, Juliette Kenny, prompting her family to express their "immeasurable loss" and call for enhanced protection for young p...

item.title: Tanzania engages Chinese investors to develop local textile industry and create jobs
item.description: Tanzania iko serious sana ku attract investment ya China, hasa kwenye textile sector. Prof Kitila Mkumbo, Minister of State in the President’s Office, Planning and Investment, alikwenda China kuzungum...

item.title: Stakeholders Discuss Electricity Reliability and Governance Concerns in SOCOTECO II Service Areas

item.title: What travellers should know as conflict disrupts global air travel and drives up costs
item.description: Global air travel has been significantly disrupted by the ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel and Iran, which has spread across much of the Middle East. The situation has led to missi...

item.title: Zamfara Governor Calls for Unity and Prayers for Peace During Eid-el-Fitr Celebration
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don call on Muslim faithful to increase their prayers for peace as dem celebrate Eid-el-Fitr, wey mark the end of the Ramadan fasting period. As Muslims complete ...

item.title: Australian PM Albanese confronted by protesters at Eid prayers over Gaza stance
item.description: During Eidul Fitr prayers at Lakemba Mosque, the largest mosque in Australia, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese was heckled and booed by protesters upset with his government's position on Israel's milit...
